heroHeadline: KIA Drones Strike Junta Hilltop in Hpakant; Three Civilians Tortured and Executed in Sagaing; NUG Vows Revolution Continues as SAC Opens ASEAN Outreach
heroSubtitle: On April 11, KIA and allied KPDF forces struck a junta strategic hilltop in Hpakant (Kachin State) with drone bombs at noon; the SAC retaliated within 30 minutes by bombing civilian Hmawsi Khtaung ward and Cemetery Hill. Three civilians were found tortured and executed in Chaungkan Village, Pale Township (Sagaing) on April 12 after being abducted as human shields by SAC forces. The Arakan Army's commander declared decisive battles must be won before 2027 as the Sittwe siege tightens to within 2 km of the city center. NUG spokesperson Nay Phone Latt rejected Min Aung Hlaing's April 10 presidential inauguration: 'The Myanmar people do not accept it. The revolution will continue with great momentum.' On April 10, joint resistance forces killed 8 SAC troops in the Ngape Township Kinpwonchaung Gate raid; the Launglone Steel Butterfly Force killed 2 more in a Launglon drone strike. Sustained airstrikes since April 7 have dropped ~40 bombs on Bilin Township (Mon State), destroying telecom infrastructure. Min Aung Hlaing met delegations from Laos, Vietnam, Cambodia, and Nicaragua on April 11, signaling ASEAN normalization momentum.
footerNote: Data sourced from AAPP, ACLED, UN OCHA, Irrawaddy, RFA, Myanmar Now, DVB, Al Jazeera, Mizzima, Human Rights Watch, Crisis Group, Myanmar Peace Monitor, MoeMaKa, IMNA/Mon News, and official NUG, KNU, KIA, and AA statements.
